ComprehendMedicalAsyncJobPropertiesList.description: A list containing the properties of each job returned.

NextToken.description: Identifies the next page of results to return.

JobId.description: The identifier assigned to the detection job.

JobName.description: The name that you assigned to the detection job.

JobStatus.description: The current status of the detection job. If the status is FAILED, the Message field shows the reason for the failure.

Message.description: A description of the status of a job.

SubmitTime.description: The time that the detection job was submitted for processing.

EndTime.description: The time that the detection job completed.

ExpirationTime.description: The date and time that job metadata is deleted from the server. Output files in your S3 bucket will not be deleted. After the metadata is deleted, the job will no longer appear in the results of the ListEntitiesDetectionV2Job or the ListPHIDetectionJobs operation.

InputDataConfig.description: The input data configuration that you supplied when you created the detection job.

S3Bucket.description: The URI of the S3 bucket that contains the input data. The bucket must be in the same region as the API endpoint that you are calling.

S3Key.description: The path to the input data files in the S3 bucket.

OutputDataConfig.description: The output data configuration that you supplied when you created the detection job.

S3Bucket.description: When you use the OutputDataConfig object with asynchronous operations, you specify the Amazon S3 location where you want to write the output data. The URI must be in the same region as the API endpoint that you are calling. The location is used as the prefix for the actual location of the output.

S3Key.description: The path to the output data files in the S3 bucket. Amazon Comprehend Medical creates an output directory using the job ID so that the output from one job does not overwrite the output of another.

LanguageCode.description: The language code of the input documents.

DataAccessRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that gives Amazon Comprehend Medical read access to your input data.

ManifestFilePath.description: The path to the file that describes the results of a batch job.

KMSKey.description: The AWS Key Management Service key, if any, used to encrypt the output files.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents. The version number looks like X.X.X. You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents. The version number looks like X.X.X. You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

Entities.description: The collection of medical entities extracted from the input text and their associated information. For each entity, the response provides the entity text, the entity category, where the entity text begins and ends, and the level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the detection and analysis. Attributes and traits of the entity are also returned.

UnmappedAttributes.description: Attributes extracted from the input text that we were unable to relate to an entity.

PaginationToken.description: If the result of the previous request to DetectEntities was truncated, include the PaginationToken to fetch the next page of entities.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents. The version number looks like X.X.X. You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

Id.description: The numeric identifier for the entity. This is a monotonically increasing id unique within this response rather than a global unique identifier.

BeginOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity begins. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

EndOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity ends. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

Score.description: The level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the accuracy of the detection.

Text.description: The segment of input text extracted as this entity.

Type.description: Describes the specific type of entity with category of entities.

Attributes.description: The extracted attributes that relate to this entity.

Entities.description: The collection of medical entities extracted from the input text and their associated information. For each entity, the response provides the entity text, the entity category, where the entity text begins and ends, and the level of confidence in the detection and analysis. Attributes and traits of the entity are also returned.

UnmappedAttributes.description: Attributes extracted from the input text that couldn't be related to an entity.

PaginationToken.description: If the result to the DetectEntitiesV2 operation was truncated, include the PaginationToken to fetch the next page of entities.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents. The version number looks like X.X.X. You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

Id.description: The numeric identifier for the entity. This is a monotonically increasing id unique within this response rather than a global unique identifier.

BeginOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity begins. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

EndOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity ends. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

Score.description: The level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the accuracy of the detection.

Text.description: The segment of input text extracted as this entity.

Type.description: Describes the specific type of entity with category of entities.

Attributes.description: The extracted attributes that relate to this entity.

Entities.description: The collection of PHI entities extracted from the input text and their associated information. For each entity, the response provides the entity text, the entity category, where the entity text begins and ends, and the level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in its detection.

PaginationToken.description: If the result of the previous request to DetectPHI was truncated, include the PaginationToken to fetch the next page of PHI entities.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents. The version number looks like X.X.X. You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

Id.description: The numeric identifier for the entity. This is a monotonically increasing id unique within this response rather than a global unique identifier.

BeginOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity begins. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

EndOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity ends. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

Score.description: The level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the accuracy of the detection.

Text.description: The segment of input text extracted as this entity.

Type.description: Describes the specific type of entity with category of entities.

Attributes.description: The extracted attributes that relate to this entity.

Entities.description: The medical conditions detected in the text linked to ICD-10-CM concepts. If the action is successful, the service sends back an HTTP 200 response, as well as the entities detected.

PaginationToken.description: If the result of the previous request to InferICD10CM was truncated, include the PaginationToken to fetch the next page of medical condition entities.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents, in the format n.n.n You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

Id.description: The numeric identifier for the entity. This is a monotonically increasing id unique within this response rather than a global unique identifier.

Text.description: The segment of input text that is matched to the detected entity.

Category.description: The category of the entity. InferICD10CM detects entities in the MEDICAL_CONDITION category.

Type.description: Describes the specific type of entity with category of entities. InferICD10CM detects entities of the type DX_NAME and TIME_EXPRESSION.

Score.description: The level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the accuracy of the detection.

BeginOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity begins. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

EndOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity ends. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

Attributes.description: The detected attributes that relate to the entity. An extracted segment of the text that is an attribute of an entity, or otherwise related to an entity, such as the nature of a medical condition.

Traits.description: Provides Contextual information for the entity. The traits recognized by InferICD10CM are DIAGNOSIS, SIGN, SYMPTOM, and NEGATION.

ICD10CMConcepts.description: The ICD-10-CM concepts that the entity could refer to, along with a score indicating the likelihood of the match.

Entities.description: The medication entities detected in the text linked to RxNorm concepts. If the action is successful, the service sends back an HTTP 200 response, as well as the entities detected.

PaginationToken.description: If the result of the previous request to InferRxNorm was truncated, include the PaginationToken to fetch the next page of medication entities.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents, in the format n.n.n You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

Id.description: The numeric identifier for the entity. This is a monotonically increasing id unique within this response rather than a global unique identifier.

Text.description: The segment of input text extracted from which the entity was detected.

Category.description: The category of the entity. The recognized categories are GENERIC or BRAND_NAME.

Type.description: Describes the specific type of entity. For InferRxNorm, the recognized entity type is MEDICATION.

Score.description: The level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the accuracy of the detected entity.

BeginOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity begins. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

EndOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity ends. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

Attributes.description: The extracted attributes that relate to the entity. The attributes recognized by InferRxNorm are DOSAGE, DURATION, FORM, FREQUENCY, RATE, ROUTE_OR_MODE, and STRENGTH.

RxNormConcepts.description: The RxNorm concepts that the entity could refer to, along with a score indicating the likelihood of the match.

Entities.description: The collection of medical concept entities extracted from the input text and their associated information. For each entity, the response provides the entity text, the entity category, where the entity text begins and ends, and the level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the detection and analysis. Attributes and traits of the entity are also returned.

PaginationToken.description: If the result of the request is truncated, the pagination token can be used to fetch the next page of entities.

ModelVersion.description: The version of the model used to analyze the documents, in the format n.n.n You can use this information to track the model used for a particular batch of documents.

SNOMEDCTDetails.description: The details of the SNOMED-CT revision, including the edition, language, and version date.

Characters.description: The number of characters in the input request documentation.

Id.description: The numeric identifier for the entity. This is a monotonically increasing id unique within this response rather than a global unique identifier.

Text.description: The segment of input text extracted as this entity.

Category.description: The category of the detected entity. Possible categories are MEDICAL_CONDITION, ANATOMY, or TEST_TREATMENT_PROCEDURE.

Type.description: Describes the specific type of entity with category of entities. Possible types include DX_NAME, ACUITY, DIRECTION, SYSTEM_ORGAN_SITE, TEST_NAME, TEST_VALUE, TEST_UNIT, PROCEDURE_NAME, or TREATMENT_NAME.

Score.description: The level of confidence that Amazon Comprehend Medical has in the accuracy of the detected entity.

BeginOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity begins. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

EndOffset.description: The 0-based character offset in the input text that shows where the entity ends. The offset returns the UTF-8 code point in the string.

Attributes.description: An extracted segment of the text that is an attribute of an entity, or otherwise related to an entity, such as the dosage of a medication taken.

SNOMEDCTConcepts.description: The SNOMED concepts that the entity could refer to, along with a score indicating the likelihood of the match.

JobId.description: The identifier generated for the job. To get the status of a job, use this identifier with the DescribeEntitiesDetectionV2Job operation.
The identifier generated for the job. To get the status of a job, use this identifier with the StartICD10CMInferenceJob operation.
